---
title: "Reproducible Analytical Process"
subtitle: "DIPEL-IMARPE analysis template"
author: "Dirección de Investigaciones del Subsistema Pelágico"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
  docx:
    toc: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Purpose

This document is a minimal analytical report generated from a reproducible pipeline.

The template should be adapted for real analytical processes such as CPUE standardization, selectivity analysis, survey indicators, environmental indicators, or spatio-temporal fisheries workflows.

# Pipeline

```{r}
#| label: pipeline-status
if (requireNamespace("targets", quietly = TRUE)) {
  targets::tar_visnetwork(targets_only = TRUE)
} else {
  message("Install the targets package to visualize the pipeline.")
}
```

# Input data

```{r}
#| label: read-targets
if (requireNamespace("targets", quietly = TRUE) && file.exists("_targets.R")) {
  clean_data <- targets::tar_read(clean_data)
  summary_table <- targets::tar_read(summary_table)
  diagnostic_table <- targets::tar_read(diagnostic_table)
} else {
  source("scripts/06_export.R")
}
```

```{r}
#| label: preview-data
head(clean_data)
```

# Summary table

```{r}
#| label: summary-table
summary_table
```

# Model diagnostics

```{r}
#| label: diagnostics
 diagnostic_table
```

# Figure

```{r}
#| label: cpue-trend
#| fig-cap: "Example CPUE trend generated by the template pipeline."
knitr::include_graphics("outputs/figures/cpue_trend.png")
```

# Outputs

The pipeline exports:

- `outputs/tables/summary_table.csv`
- `outputs/tables/diagnostics.csv`
- `outputs/figures/cpue_trend.png`
- `outputs/models/model_fit.rds`

# Notes for adaptation

Replace the synthetic dataset, functions, and model with the real analytical process. Keep the numbered scripts and `_targets.R` synchronized.
